The following error originated from your application code, not from Cypress. It was caused by an unhandled promise rejection.

  > Request failed with status code 400

When Cypress detects uncaught errors originating from your application it will automatically fail the current test.

This behavior is configurable, and you can choose to turn this off by listening to the `uncaught:exception` event.

https://on.cypress.io/uncaught-exception-from-application
Timed out retrying after 30000ms: Expected to find content: 'Test Branch 1777570771856' within the selector: 'tr' but never did.
Timed out retrying after 30000ms: Expected to find content: 'Test Branch 1777570771856 Updated' within the selector: 'tr' but never did.

Timed out retrying after 30050ms: `cy.click()` failed because the page updated while this command was executing. Cypress tried to locate elements based on this query:

> cy.get(button).contains(Add New)

We initially found matching element(s), but while waiting for them to become actionable, they disappeared from the page. Common situations why this happens:
  - Your JS framework re-rendered asynchronously
  - Your app code reacted to an event firing and removed the element

You can typically solve this by breaking up a chain. For example, rewrite:

> `cy.get('button').click().click()`

to

> `cy.get('button').as('btn').click()`
> `cy.get('@btn').click()`

https://on.cypress.io/element-has-detached-from-dom
Timed out retrying after 30000ms: Expected to find content: 'parasoli7379+1777570941229@gmail.com' within the selector: 'tr' but never did.
